{"step_by_step_thinking": "The query asks about afferent nerve fibers of pulp. The relevant information can be found in Document [12], which discusses the sympathetic adrenergic vasomotor control of the pulp. It mentions that the cervical sympathetic ganglion conveys sympathetic nerve fibers, which join the trigeminal nerve ganglion and branch to either follow the path of the sensory nerve fibers from the teeth or travel alongside the blood vessels. This indicates that the afferent nerve fibers of pulp are sympathetic postganglionic fibers.", "answer_choice": "B"}
